data.title: Aware outlines 2026 focus on pipeline conversion and biometric platform scaling amid foundational progress
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Aware, Inc. (AWRE) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ajay Amlani indicated that fiscal 2025 was a "foundational year" for Aware, marked by "meaningful strategic progress across our technology platform, leadership team, certifications and market positioning." He emphasized progress in three transformation pillars: advancing core biometric technology (with a focus on liveness and biometric orchestration), strengthening a customer-obsessed go-to-market model, and deepening partnerships and certifications.
  • Amlani stated that Aware’s next-generation Intelligent Liveness technology "combines deep biometric expertise with advanced spoof protection to deliver...verifiable proof of personhood" and highlighted best-in-class results in NIST IR 8491 evaluations and fast, adaptive capture speeds.
  • He described ongoing investments in open architecture for the Awareness Platform, allowing modular integration and elimination of vendor lock-in. "We are focused on building out our open architecture biometric infrastructure to bring civil and criminal identity management together in a single secure and highly scalable environment," said Amlani.
  • Amlani announced deployments including the first mobile biometric solution within a U.S. federal agency and a pilot program with a Caribbean nation for government employee time and attendance systems. He also mentioned a successful biometric boarding test at Orlando International Airport in partnership with the Greater Orlando Aviation Authority and the Department of Homeland Security.
  • Leadership changes included a new Head of Engineering and a new Head of Product, both introduced to drive operational improvements and better align product development with customer needs.
  • The company achieved multiple certifications, such as ISO-30107 Level 3 for Presentation Attack Detection, ISO 27001 for information security management, and FIDO2 Server Certification, which "materially strengthen our credibility with both enterprise and government buyers" (Amlani).
  • CFO David Traverse stated, "Revenue in the fourth quarter was $4.7 million compared to $4.8 million in the prior year period. The slight decrease reflects lower perpetual software license revenue, partially offset by higher maintenance and services and other revenue."
  • Traverse added, "Operating expenses for the quarter improved to $6.1 million compared to $6.3 million in the prior year quarter. The lower expenses largely reflects the onetime costs incurred in the prior year period related to the former CEO's transition, which includes severance and acceleration of stock-based compensation expense of $600,000."

Outlook

  • Management expects ongoing variability in quarterly results due to procurement cycles and customer conversion timing, particularly in government and large enterprise markets. Traverse stated, "We believe we will continue to experience quarterly results that remain uneven given the nature of our procurement cycles and customer conversion timing."
  • Amlani noted, "Execution and conversion will take time, and we expect variability to remain part of the near-term landscape. However, we believe the structural progress achieved over the past year strengthens our competitive position and supports our long-term opportunity in biometric identity."
  • For 2026, Amlani advised investors to focus on "improving execution, stronger conversion of our pipeline into actual program wins, recurring software deployments, larger solution-based programs, particularly in government markets," as well as progression from pilots to production deployments.

Financial Results

  • Traverse reported a net loss for the quarter of $1.5 million or $0.07 per diluted share, compared to a net loss of $1.2 million or $0.06 per diluted share in the prior year quarter.
  • Adjusted EBITDA loss was $800,000 for both Q4 2025 and the prior year quarter.
  • Year-end cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities were $22.3 million with no debt. Full-year revenue was $17.3 million compared to $17.4 million in 2024. Net loss for the year was $5.9 million or $0.28 per diluted share compared to $4.4 million or $0.21 per diluted share in the previous year. Adjusted EBITDA loss for the year was $4.6 million compared to $3.9 million in the prior period.

Q&A

  • Analyst: Federal procurement timing has created some variability in results and 2025 revenue was essentially flat year-over-year. How should investors think about the drivers of potential growth going forward, particularly given the mix of federal, commercial and international opportunities? Amlani: "In 2025, we did see some programs move slower than expected during the year...Coming out of that, we've seen increased activity in federal government meetings, which have resulted in significant pipeline acceleration and opportunities here in the U.S. federal government work."
  • Analyst: How should investors think about the mix between perpetual licenses, recurring software, services and maintenance going forward? Traverse: "The mix will likely continue to evolve depending on the types of programs we're secure...rather than targeting a specific mix, what we're focused on is expanding our presence in these large programs where our software platform can be deployed and maintained over multiple years."
  • Analyst: What is the process and time line from evaluation to production deployment? Amlani: "These evaluations can basically move on to pilot programs and then the customers will validate the technology within their environment to decide if they want to be able to move forward...our goal is to start small...and then continuously exceeding those expectations."
  • Analyst: How did the recent certifications and platform enhancements strengthen Aware's competitive position? Amlani: "These certifications are typically listed within procurement from major customers...being able to meet these certifications, puts us in a competitive set that's smaller than the more broader set."
  • Analyst: Are you seeing any new AI native competitors or customers in-sourcing by building their own algorithms using LLMs? What moats or risks does your business have from an AI disruption? Amlani: "Our company is actually really well positioned to be able to take advantage of the LLMs and the technology...Our existing presence in the market with existing customers and data allows us to be able to work with that data to be able to train our models in a more effective fashion."
  • Analyst: What should investors look for in 2026 to measure success? Amlani: "The proof points that investors should look around or look for really around improving execution, stronger conversion of our pipeline into actual program wins, recurring software deployments, larger solution-based programs, particularly in government markets."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' tone was neutral to slightly cautious, with repeated questions about revenue variability, pipeline conversion, and the impact of federal procurement cycles.
  • Management maintained a neutral but disciplined tone, acknowledging variability and emphasizing foundational progress and long-term opportunity. Amlani used phrases such as "we believe the structural progress achieved over the past year strengthens our competitive position."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both analysts and management continued to focus on variability and pipeline conversion, with no significant change in sentiment.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained focused on variability and the need for multi-quarter evaluation due to procurement cycles, consistent with Q3.
  • Strategic focus shifted further toward certifications and platform enhancements, and execution on large government and international opportunities.
  • Analysts continued to press on near-term revenue sustainability and growth drivers, mirroring previous quarter concerns.
  • Key metric changes included a sequential decline in quarterly revenue from $5.1 million in Q3 to $4.7 million in Q4, with a slight increase in net loss.
  • Management’s confidence in long-term opportunity and foundational progress was reiterated, with emphasis on improved platform capabilities and certifications.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited continued variability in quarterly results, especially due to government procurement cycles and customer conversion timing.
  • Delayed appropriations and procurement cycles, especially related to the government shutdown, were highlighted as factors affecting timing and revenue realization.
  • Analysts focused on sustainability of growth, mix of revenue streams, and risks from AI-native competitors and customer in-sourcing, which management addressed by emphasizing proprietary data and technology adaptability.

Final Takeaway

Aware’s fourth quarter and full-year 2025 results reflected ongoing variability in revenue tied to federal procurement cycles, yet the company highlighted expanded certifications, leadership additions, and strategic platform upgrades as key advances. Management stressed that 2025 laid the groundwork for future growth, with investors advised to look for improved pipeline conversion, recurring software deployments, and execution on government and international opportunities in 2026, as Aware aims to transition foundational progress into scalable, predictable results.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: OmniAb outlines $25M–$30M 2026 revenue guidance as partner pipeline accelerates with OmniUltra and xPloration launches
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: OmniAb (OABI) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Matthew Foehr reported building momentum throughout 2025, especially in expanding both the roster of partners and the number of active programs enabled by the company’s technologies. "By year-end, we're happy to report that we had 107 partners who are running 407 active programs." Foehr emphasized the introduction of OmniUltra at the Antibody Engineering Conference, calling it "the industry's first and only transgenic chicken platform to express ultra-long CDRH3 on a human antibody framework." He highlighted OmniUltra as a growth driver aimed at gaining new partners and increasing near-term revenue opportunities, while the xPloration platform is designed to bring high-throughput single B-cell screening directly into partner labs, expected to be "additive to the business and to contribute to our growth." Foehr also stated, "our differentiated platforms and business are highly scalable, allowing us to add new programs while maintaining operating efficiency, positioning OmniAb on a sustainable path to future growth."
  • CFO Kurt Gustafson noted a revenue decrease for the quarter but pointed out cost discipline: "Our operating expenses in the fourth quarter decreased to $24.1 million from $26.7 million. Most of this decrease was due to lower personnel costs, but we also saw lower outside service costs." Gustafson also highlighted a noncash impairment charge of $3.9 million. He added, "We ended the year with $54 million in cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments."

Outlook

  • Gustafson provided 2026 guidance: "We expect revenue in 2026 to be in the range of $25 million to $30 million. We expect operating expense to be in the range of $80 million to $85 million as we continue to realize efficiencies in the business." Cash operating expense is expected to be $50 million to $55 million, with the company anticipating an end-of-year cash balance between $30 million and $35 million. "2026 full year effective tax rate is expected to be approximately 0% due to the valuation allowance."
  • The long-term financial outlook anticipates a shift from milestone-driven to royalty-driven revenue, with $3 billion in contracted milestones and an average royalty rate of 3.4%.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter total revenue was reported as $8.4 million, compared with $10.8 million in the same period in 2024. The decrease was primarily attributed to lower license revenue, partially offset by an increase in milestone revenue and a small contribution from xPloration.
  • Operating expenses for Q4 2025 decreased to $24.1 million from $26.7 million. Net loss for the quarter was $14.2 million or $0.11 per share, compared to a net loss of $13.1 million or $0.12 per share in the prior year period.
  • Full year 2025 revenue reached $18.7 million versus $26.4 million in 2024. Operating expense for the year was $87.6 million, down from $100.9 million last year. Net loss for 2025 was $64.8 million or $0.57 per share.

Q&A

  • Puneet Souda, Leerink Partners LLC, Research Division: Asked about the impact of market fundraising activity on new program growth and feedback from the business side. Foehr said, "we observed really nice momentum in program additions and also a really strong year in terms of partner adds as well... we feel like we're very well positioned for this year with coming on the tail-end of new technology launches."
  • Souda followed up on xPloration revenue growth and pull-through. Foehr responded that the company expects significant growth for xPloration and contribution this year but hasn't broken down the revenue guidance by product.
  • Michael King inquired about cash flow neutrality versus investment in partnerships. Foehr explained that the company can efficiently add incremental partnerships and that "most of our R&D costs relate to maintaining our animal colonies with a small amount directed towards new technology development."
  • King asked about share count increases. Gustafson clarified, "We did raise some capital, and so that raising capital increased the share count during that period of time."
  • Matt Hewitt, Craig-Hallum Capital Group LLC, Research Division: Asked about the number of xPloration systems deployed. Foehr said, "2, instruments deployed as of the end of 2025," and expects growth in 2026. Hewitt also asked about breakeven timing; Foehr declined to specify a date but stated confidence in the trajectory toward cash flow positivity.
  • Joseph Pantginis, H.C. Wainwright & Co, LLC, Research Division: Queried xPloration's manufacturing needs. Gustafson said, "there's not a large incremental investment in terms of staff," and instruments are built to order, keeping the program lean.
  • Chad Wiatrowski, TD Cowen, Research Division: Asked about the OmniUltra launch response. Foehr described it as "going fantastically well" with increasing partner engagement and momentum. Wiatrowski also questioned 2026 revenue guidance's return to 2024 levels. Gustafson attributed it to milestone-based revenue timing rather than market recovery.
  • Anna for Kripa, Tourist: Asked about xPloration's role in attracting new partners. Foehr said xPloration deepens relationships with existing partners and attracts new ones. Anna also asked about OmniUltra milestones; Foehr expects initial focus to be on adding partners and programs.
  • Stephen Willey, Stifel: Asked about royalty structures. Gustafson and Foehr indicated most deals are flat royalties, with some tiered, and that innovative technologies can drive better economics.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts showed a neutral to slightly positive tone, focusing on growth drivers, new technology launches, and revenue contributions, while seeking clarification on cash flow and revenue breakdowns. Questions on xPloration, OmniUltra, and financial discipline reflected curiosity but not skepticism.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone, emphasizing scalability, innovation, and a trajectory toward positive cash flow. Foehr frequently highlighted new technology launches and partner momentum, with no defensive or evasive language detected.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone remains upbeat, with increased emphasis on innovation and scalability. Both analysts and management displayed heightened interest in growth prospects and efficiency.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company increased its active partners from 104 to 107 and active programs from 399 to 407, with a net addition of 44 programs for the year.
  • New technology launches, particularly OmniUltra and xPloration, took center stage, with tangible progress in partner engagement and early revenue contribution.
  • Management's tone shifted from cautious optimism about OmniUltra's upcoming launch in Q3 to clear confidence in early traction and momentum in Q4.
  • Analysts shifted focus from questions about cash runway and private placements in Q3 to specific queries about new product traction, partner engagement, and growth outlook in Q4.
  • The Q4 transcript included more detailed discussion of milestone-based revenue variability and operational efficiency.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted revenue variability due to milestone-driven contracts, with Gustafson stating, "the revenue number is lumpy."
  • Attrition and program regression were acknowledged as normal, with 40 program terminations and 4 regressions in 2025.
  • A noncash impairment charge of $3.9 million was recognized, primarily related to small molecule ion channel property and equipment.
  • Analyst concerns focused on xPloration scale-up, potential for new program growth amid market conditions, and timing of achieving cash flow breakeven, all addressed by management as manageable within current strategy.

Final Takeaway

OmniAb management highlighted a year of expanding partnerships, robust program growth, and the successful introduction of innovative platforms such as OmniUltra and xPloration. The company projects 2026 revenue between $25 million and $30 million while maintaining a disciplined cost structure and clear trajectory toward positive cash flow. Management underscored the scalability of OmniAb’s business model and increasing royalty streams as key drivers for long-term value creation, all supported by a growing and diversifying partner base and continued investment in differentiated technology platforms.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Asia markets bounce back: KOSPI soars 11% in historic rebound, China pledges stimulus despite low growth goal
data.description:
Asian stocks increased significantly on Thursday, led by South Korea's KOSPI recovering from losses due to bargain buying and positive signals from Wall Street. Chinese stocks also rose as Beijing announced a weaker growth target for 2026 while promising continued fiscal support.

Bitcoin (BTC-USD) held above $72,000 on Thursday, trading near a four-week high, as markets recover from volatility linked to the ongoing Middle East war.

Gold rose to about $5,170 per ounce on Thursday, building on the prior session’s advance as investors sought safety amid escalating tensions in the Middle East. 

South Korea's KOSPI index (KOSPI) surged 11% to above 5,600 on Thursday after tumbling nearly 20% over the previous three sessions, as semiconductor and broader technology shares bounced back from the historic selloff. The South Korean won steadied around 1,461 per dollar, halting a sharp selloff in the previous session that had driven it to a 17-year low.

Japan (NKY:IND) rose 2.24% to above 56,500, while the broader Topix advanced 3.5% to 3,760 on Thursday, recovering losses from earlier in the week. The Japanese yen strengthened past 157 per dollar on Thursday, extending gains from the previous session.

Japan’s 10-year government bond yield climbed nearly 5 basis points to around 2.15% on Thursday following a well-received 30-year government bond sale, despite heightened uncertainty from the Middle East conflict. 

China (SHCOMP) rose 0.80% to around 4,100, while the Shenzhen Component gained 1.5% to 14,130 on Thursday, breaking a two-day decline, and the offshore yuan steadied around 6.89 per dollar on Thursday, as a stronger daily fixing helped offset cautious market sentiment following Beijing’s newly lowered economic growth target. 

At the opening of Thursday’s National People’s Congress, Premier Li Qiang unveiled a GDP growth target of 4.5% to 5% for 2026. This modest goal signals a definitive new chapter of "sober growth," marking the most restrained economic target Beijing has set since the early 1990s as it prioritizes structural stability over rapid expansion.

China’s 10-year government bond yield rose to around 1.79% on Thursday, rebounding from a near three-week low earlier this week, as investors weighed the country’s revised GDP growth goal. 

Separately, China pledged to stabilize its property sector and optimize housing supply, according to an official government report cited by Reuters. Authorities plan to better utilize existing commodity housing stock, including purchasing unsold homes and converting them into government-subsidized housing to reduce excess inventory.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose 0.60% to 25,679 in early Thursday trade, snapping a three-day slide.

India (SENSEX) rose 0.65% to 79,381 on Thursday morning trade, bouncing back from a four-day losing streak as most sectors moved into positive territory, except for technology, consumer non-durables, and commercial services, which posted modest declines. The Indian rupee rose to around 91.5 per dollar, rebounding after sliding to a record low in the previous session.

Australia (AS51) rose 0.44% to around 8,900 in Thursday morning deals, after shedding more than 3% over the previous two sessions. The Australian dollar hovered around $0.706, trading in a narrow range near three-year peaks, as investors weighed the impact and duration of the ongoing Middle East conflict.

Australia’s goods trade surplus dropped to AUD 2.63 billion in January 2026, down from AUD 3.37 billion in December and below the expected AUD 3.9 billion. This decline was due to lower exports, which fell 0.9% to AUD 44.06 billion, and a 0.8% rise in imports to AUD 41.43 billion, indicating stronger domestic demand at the start of the year.

Further data out today showed household consumption rose 0.3% month-on-month in January, following a 0.5% fall in December but falling short of expectations for a 0.4% increase, pointing to a patchy recovery. 

In the U.S. on Wednesday, all three major indexes ended higher, with investors looking past Middle East tensions. The Dow added 0.5 percentage points as a retreat in oil prices and robust economic data calmed growth fears. 

U.S. stock futures held steady on Thursday after the major averages staged a rebound in the previous session, driven primarily by strength in technology and semiconductor shares: Dow -0.33%; S&P 500 -0.25%; Nasdaq -0.26%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia's markets and economy:

data.title: OpenAI explored ad sales with The Trade Desk as revenue hits $25B: The ⁠Information
data.description:
• OpenAI (OPENAI) has held early talks to partner with The Trade Desk (TTD) to help it sell advertising, as it leans on external partners to ramp up its business, The Information reported on Wednesday.
  • TTD (TTD) shares rose 9.3% in Wednesday’s after-market session; ad-tech peer AppLovin’s (APP) stock rose more than 1%. OpenAI is also reportedly in talks with other brands, media agencies, and ad tech firms as part of the early ads pilot.
  • The Sam Altman-led company has recently said that it was beginning to test ads in ChatGPT in the U.S. The test will be for logged-in adult users on the Free and Go subscription tiers. 
  • According to a separate report, the artificial intelligence startup topped $25 billion in annualized revenue as of the ‌end of last month. This is a ​17% increase from the $21.4 billion in annualized revenue that the company generated at the end of the year, the report said.

data.title: Meta eyes in-house chips to train AI models - report
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) plans to expand its custom silicon efforts beyond applications for simple recommendation algorithms to eventually support complex AI model training, according to a Bloomberg report that cites the company CFO's comments from a recent conference.

The company believes some of its workloads, especially those tied to AI models and personalized recommendations, are customized, and building its own chips could improve performance and efficiency. The strategy aims to tailor hardware to Meta's (META) unique needs, reducing reliance on third-party suppliers like Nvidia (NVDA) and AMD (AMD).

“Some of our workloads really are very customized to us,” Meta CFO Susan Li commented at a technology conference. “The sort of ranking and recommendations workloads have been where we have started, and that’s the place where we have rolled out custom silicon at the most scale. But we expect and are hopeful that we are going to expand that over time, including eventually to training AI models.” he added.

The testing of Meta's (META) first in-house AI training chip began in early 2025, and executives had earlier targeted 2026 rollout for generative AI, though its advanced designs like Olympus faced setbacks in February.

Still, Meta (META) prioritizes proprietary silicon for customized workloads, with the move aligning with broader industry trends to optimize AI infrastructure costs and performance.

data.title: China signals era of 'sober growth' sets lowest GDP target since 1991 at 4.5%–5%
data.description:
At the opening of Thursday’s National People’s Congress, Premier Li Qiang unveiled a GDP growth target of 4.5% to 5% for 2026. This modest goal signals a definitive new chapter of "sober growth," marking the most restrained economic target Beijing has set since the early 1990s as it prioritizes structural stability over rapid expansion.

This cautious forecast indicates that policymakers are more open to accepting slower growth while focusing on sustainable economic drivers and reducing dependence on debt for property and infrastructure.

During the annual legislative session, Premier Li Qiang stated the government wants to maintain growth momentum after achieving its 2025 target, aided by strong exports despite trade issues with the U. S.

It acknowledges that the growth model of the past 40 years is under pressure. 

Meanwhile, the budget deficit will remain at around 4% of GDP, unchanged from 2025, with a consumer inflation target of around 2.0% to prevent deflation and to support growth amid domestic challenges and rising geopolitical risks. 

Stabilizing employment remains a priority; the government aims to create 12 million urban jobs and keep the unemployment rate at 5.5%, unchanged from 2025. 

In addition, local governments will issue CNY 4.4 trillion in special-purpose bonds for investments, and CNY 1.3 trillion in treasury bonds will also be issued to reinforce fiscal support.

Separately, China pledged to stabilize its property sector and optimize housing supply, according to an official government report cited by Reuters. Authorities plan to better utilize existing commodity housing stock, including purchasing unsold homes and converting them into government-subsidized housing to reduce excess inventory.

On the external front, mainland stocks tracked a rebound in global markets as inflationary concerns eased, though hostilities between the US and Iran persisted.

On Thursday, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.5% to around 4,100, while the Shenzhen Component gained 1.5% to 14,130 on Thursday, breaking a two-day decline, and the offshore yuan steadied around 6.89 per dollar on Thursday, as a stronger daily fixing helped offset cautious market sentiment following Beijing’s newly lowered economic growth target. 

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXI), (NYSEARCA:KWEB), (NYSEARCA:CQQQ), (NASDAQ:MCHI), (NYSEARCA:ASHR), (NYSEARCA:YINN), (NYSE:TDF), (NYSEARCA:CHIQ), (NYSEARCA:GXC), (NYSEARCA:EWH), (NYSEARCA:KBA), (NYSEARCA:YANG), (NASDAQ:CXSE), (NYSE:CAF), (NYSEARCA:CWEB), (NASDAQ:PGJ), (NYSEARCA:KURE).

Currency: (CNY:USD)

data.title: Venezuela’s state‑owned miner signs deal to sell 1,000 kg of gold to Trafigura; eyes U.S. mining cooperation
data.description:
Venezuela's state-owned mining company on Monday inked a multimillion-dollar deal to sell as many as 1,000 kilograms of gold destined for U.S. markets, Axios reported on Wednesday. The deal requires Venezuelan mining company ​Minerven to furnish ​650 to 1,000 kg of gold ‌dore ⁠bars to Trafigura, the report said, adding that Trafigura will ship the gold to ​U.S. ​refineries ⁠under a separate arrangement with the U.S. ​government.

The contract calls for 98% final gold content, the source said. U.S. Interior Secretary Doug Burgum, who arrived in Venezuela Wednesday to discuss oil and mineral opportunities, helped shepherd the gold contract.

Gold prices (XAUUSD:CUR) have already climbed nearly 20% this year, after hitting a record high above $5,595 per ounce in late January, as global uncertainty and safe-haven demand kept bullion markets firm.

This contract is the third extraction deal made under the Trump administration's supervision as the U.S. has taken control of Venezuela's most crucial and abundant resource, oil, the report said. Trafigura is also involved in those oil contracts worth more than $1 billion.

Separatetly, Venezuela's interim President Delcy Rodriguez said on Wednesday a reform of the country's main mining law will be submitted in coming days to the country's legislature, after a ​meeting in Caracas with Burgum.

Burgum, who also heads the U.S. National Energy Dominance Council, arrived in the South American country with more than two dozen U.S. mining and minerals companies, which he said could represent billions in investment and thousands of high-paying jobs for Venezuela. Burgum is expected to meet with oil and gas ​companies on Thursday to discuss expansion and ​investment, two sources earlier told Reuters.

data.title: Australia's trade surplus shrinks to AUD 2.63B in January, missing forecasts as export momentum fades
data.description:
Australia’s goods trade surplus dropped to AUD 2.63 billion in January 2026, down from AUD 3.37 billion in December and below the expected AUD 3.9 billion.

This decline was due to lower exports, which fell 0.9% to AUD 44.06 billion, and a 0.8% rise in imports to AUD 41.43 billion, indicating stronger domestic demand at the start of the year.

In Australia, the latest data showed household spending rebounded slightly in January after falling in December, indicating softer consumer demand. Australia's trade surplus shrinks to AUD 2.63B in January, missing forecasts 

The S&P/ASX 200 Index rose 0.4% to around 8,900 in Thursday morning deals, after shedding more than 3% over the previous two sessions. The Australian dollar hovered around $0.706, trading in a narrow range near three-year peaks, as investors weighed the impact and duration of the ongoing Middle East conflict

data.title: U.S. Senate votes to back Trump on Iran strikes
data.description:
The U.S. Senate voted to block a Democratic-led resolution that would have restricted President Donald Trump's authority to conduct further military strikes on Iran without congressional approval. The action aligns with Republican support for Trump's ongoing campaign against Iran amid escalating U.S.-Israeli operations.

The Senate voted 53 to 47 not to advance the resolution. Nearly all Republicans opposed the measure, with only Sen. Rand Paul joining Democrats in favor; Sen. John Fetterman was the sole Democrat against it.

The Democrats cautioned that the administration has bypassed Congress and risked drawing the U.S. into a broader conflict without proper oversight. While the Republicans mainly defended Trump’s actions, calling them legal and necessary for U.S. national security and within the President’s authority as commander-in-chief.

"This is not a forever war, indeed not even close to it. This is going to end very quickly," Republican Senator Jim Risch of Idaho, chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, said in a speech against the resolution.

The vote comes amid an expanding conflict with Iran and Trump's airstrikes on Iranian targets, including nuclear sites and leadership, as part of "Operation Epic Fury" launched days earlier with Israel.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Thursday’s Economic Calendar
data.description:
7:30 AM Challenger Job-Cut Report 

This monthly report counts and categorizes announcements of corporate layoffs based on mass layoff data from state departments of labor. 

8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time.

Claims seen at 215K, up from 212K last week.

8:30 AM Productivity and Costs 

Productivity measures the growth of labor efficiency in producing the economy's goods and services. 

The delayed first reading for Q4 is expected to show much slower productivity growth at 1.9 percent versus 4.9 percent in Q3 and ULC up 2.1 percent versus a decrease of 1.9 percent in Q3.

8:30 AM Import and Export Prices

Import price indexes are compiled for the prices of goods that are bought in the United States but produced abroad, and export price indexes are compiled for the prices of goods sold abroad but produced domestically. 

Import prices are seen up 0.1 percent and export prices up 0.2 percent in January on the month.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country.

1:15 PM Michelle Bowman Speaks

Federal Reserve Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman speaks before the virtual New York Bankers Association "Navigating What's Next: Perspective on the Economy and Innovation" event.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.
